acceptance_targets: [ch14_award_01] Pass if a reader can point to distinct new kinds of damage in each surviving reveal beat around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:140, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:214, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:256, and if the scene's leverage visibly shifts before the current autonomy-action beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:313. Fail if the revised middle still feels like Lena clicking through multiple similarly staged proof panels until the final confirmation arrives.

[ch14_craft_01] Pass if the revised passage gives at least one explicit, text-visible reason before Lena opens the incriminating window that explains why the `eli_ward` workspace is accessible on the silver laptop despite June's existing suspicion, and if June's lapse reads as interruption-driven rather than inexplicable negligence. Fail if the reveal still rests on June casually leaving an active target thread exposed with no stated cause.

[ch14_prose_01] Pass if, after revision, the reveal passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:258, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:297 contains no more than two non-UI stand-alone fragment paragraphs, and each retained fragment lands at a distinct turning point. Fail if the scene still relies on repeated one- and two-word narration beats to manufacture intensity.

[HIGH_CH14_REVEAL_LOG_WRONG_NUMBERS] Pass if a reader cross-checking snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6553-6661 against the revised money scenes finds a one-to-one match in amount and date for each logged transfer. Fail if a stray $325, $2,400, or other unmatched figure remains in the reveal evidence.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can point to distinct new kinds of damage in each surviving reveal beat around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:140, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:214, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:256, and if the scene's leverage visibly shifts before the current autonomy-action beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:313. Fail if the revised middle still feels like Lena clicking through multiple similarly staged proof panels until the final confirmation arrives.
findings.evidence: The middle of the reveal advances through repeated module-opening beats before live scene pressure changes again: Lena clicks deeper at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:140, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:155, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:214, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:256, but the chapter does not regain a new active turn until the autonomy message at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:313.
findings.problem: The reveal is legible and damaging, but the midsection settles into a serial click-read-recoil pattern. That keeps the chapter coherent while flattening surprise and leverage; it reads as an efficient evidence walkthrough rather than a revelation scene that keeps changing shape under Lena's hands.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the reveal corridor at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:140, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:173, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:214, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:256 so each surviving pane does a distinct dramatic job: one proves automation, one proves June's manual authorship, one proves sexual exploitation, one proves money strategy. Then bring a live-pressure shift in before or at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:313 instead of letting Lena keep discovering similarly formatted proof for that long.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage gives at least one explicit, text-visible reason before Lena opens the incriminating window that explains why the `eli_ward` workspace is accessible on the silver laptop despite June's existing suspicion, and if June's lapse reads as interruption-driven rather than inexplicable negligence. Fail if the reveal still rests on June casually leaving an active target thread exposed with no stated cause.
findings.evidence: June tells Lena to use the personal laptop at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:31 and jokes about 'weird tabs' at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:49, but that machine is already left open with a minimized live OpenClaw workspace at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:59 and a still-active `eli_ward` thread at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:79 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:98, even though June's own note says suspicion is already rising at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:205.
findings.problem: The chapter's climax depends on June making an operational mistake that is larger than the surrounding characterization currently supports. A fast, systems-competent operator who already knows Lena may be building a case would not casually hand over the same machine that still holds a live target thread unless the text gives a more concrete interruption-driven reason. Without that, the reveal risks reading as author convenience at the exact point causality needs to feel locked down.
findings.rewrite_direction: Seed a specific causal explanation into the handoff around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:31 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:49, and reinforce it when Lena sees the desktop at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:59 and the live workspace at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:79. Examples: June had to move the thread onto the personal machine because of the outage, believed she had hidden or locked it before the landlord interruption, or left only a sync notification path rather than a fully exposed workspace. The revised version needs one concrete storm-pressure detail that makes the access credible instead of lucky.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after revision, the reveal passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:258, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:297 contains no more than two non-UI stand-alone fragment paragraphs, and each retained fragment lands at a distinct turning point. Fail if the scene still relies on repeated one- and two-word narration beats to manufacture intensity.
findings.evidence: Outside of on-screen interface text, the reveal repeatedly lands on clipped fragment paragraphs such as snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:168, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:258, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:297, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:299, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:301, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:305, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:337.
findings.problem: The chapter's shock rhythm leans too often on one-line and one-word narration beats. Used this many times in a single reveal scene, the cadence starts to feel preloaded rather than earned, and several beats drift into the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping instead of staying fully grammatical and pressure-specific.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the non-UI fragment beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:167, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:168, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:258, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:297, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:299, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:301, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:305, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:337 into fuller sentences tethered to Lena's body, thought, or immediate action. Keep only the few fragment punches that buy a genuinely new turn in pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader cross-checking snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6553-6661 against the revised money scenes finds a one-to-one match in amount and date for each logged transfer. Fail if a stray $325, $2,400, or other unmatched figure remains in the reveal evidence.
findings.problem: The reveal chapter's own proof set is numerically wrong. The OpenClaw payment behavior pane logs 'sent 325 - 09/14' and 'sent 2,400 - 10/04,' which contradict the dramatized transfer scenes and also fail to align with the later archive. Because this chapter is where ambiguity ends, bad numbers here make the evidence look synthetic instead of decisive.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6553-6661, rebuild the manual-override entries and payment-behavior pane from the canonical transfer history. Preserve the damning logic and June's ugly operational notes, but stop inventing fresh amounts or dates in the moment of proof. The hidden backend should read like the secret architecture of scenes we already know, not a competing draft of them.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.
